behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is OS filesystem watcher semantics (recursive watch/event delivery from nested paths), not a single function. REAL-INFRA is required because mock events can miss platform-specific recursive-watch gaps. Rejected higher levels: behavior text has no AI/ML decision or multi-agent sequence, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real daemon process against a real temporary directory tree, create a nested subdirectory under the watched root, write a file there, and assert exactly one transformed output appears in the output directory for that nested file within timeout.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text targets deduplication logic itself (same path + time window), which is a local state/timing rule and does not require OS/network/subprocess. MOCK-MISS does not force module-boundary testing here; a unit with injected clock catches real failures in window math/path-key handling. Rejected higher levels: no required real filesystem behavior in the behavior text; no AI/ML or multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Test the dedup component directly with a fake/injected clock: submit a path once, submit again inside the configured window (expect discard), then advance time past window and submit again (expect accept).

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on OS-emitted duplicate events; this is a real watcher/runtime boundary. Mocking event sources can hide real duplicate/coalescing patterns, so System is the minimum. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependency and no chained agentic steps in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon with a real watched directory, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., temp-write + rename or repeated append/close), capture processing results, and assert only one pipeline execution/output for that file path.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is OS-level directory lifecycle behavior (watch invalidation and reattachment). In-process fakes usually do not reproduce watcher teardown/recreate edge cases, so System is required. Rejected higher levels: behavior contains no AI/ML correctness claim and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Launch daemon on a real temp watched directory, delete the directory while running, recreate it at the same path, then add a new file and assert it is processed without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on real filesystem timestamp behavior/resolution under concurrent arrivals, which is an OS/filesystem boundary. A mock clock/event stream can hide real tie-resolution and metadata timing issues. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependency and no multi-step agentic pipeline in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on a real directory, create multiple files near-simultaneously, record their filesystem creation timestamps as seen by the daemon, and assert processing/output order matches timestamp order (including deterministic tie-breaker if defined).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem write/event timing (partial writes, close/flush visibility), which requires OS-level behavior. Mocking file events or writes can always pass while hiding real partial-read races. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML quality dependency and no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon on a real watched directory, write a file in chunks with delays while keeping it open, verify processing does not start on partial content, then close/finish write and assert processing occurs afterward with complete content.
